en: Mooni, are you home?
Yes, come on in.
Mooni, are you home?

ab: Mooni omikikadhaka dha’ane! Leeve lrao, kama ikini ka dhidhape? lo oilringadha amo pato’olraidhe.
ch: B:Mooni不在家耶！我是Leeve，有什麼事嗎? 回來我會轉告Mooni。
en: Mooni is not at home. I am Leeve. What can I do for you? I will let him know when he comes back.
Who are you?

ch: B:奶奶!我們是Ahoai和Mooni，幫我們開門！
en: Grandma, we are Ahoai and Mooni, please open the door for us.
Who is it?
I am pasolrange, I am here for ’Ase.
Grandma, I am here to see you.

ch: B:你好久沒來了，我好想念你。
en: You haven’t been here for a long time. I missed you so much.
Mooni, I am here to play with you.
Come on in! I have been waiting for you for a long time.

ch: A:這是我種的地瓜，吃吃看！
en: This is the sweet potato I grow. Try it!
The sweet potato is so sweet.

ab: imiya maava’iya! ’ina ’a lredhekeli madho, loa kone.
ch: A:過來！這是我種的水果，吃看看。
en: Come here! These are the fruits I grow. You should try it.
The fruit tastes sweet.

ab: toloholrana! ’ina ’a aliyaliya’ali kiposavo’onae makatamalie loa kone!
ch: A:請坐！這是我種的有機玉米吃看看！
en: Take a seat! You should try some organic corn that I grow.
Wow! The corn tastes so sweet.
Do you want some oranges?

ab: odholrokali ka kone makataamani, kama kanengali pingko molekate.
ch: B:我不能吃橘子，我吃蘋果就好。
en: I can’t eat oranges. I will have some apples instead.
Would you want some cookies?
Sure, I like it very much.
Let me peel the fruit for you.
Thank you. This fruit is so juicy.
I will bring you some water.
Thank you.
Enjoy yourself. I cooked a lot of dishes.

ch: B:嗚! 真的很香也好吃。
en: Oh! It smells good and looks delicious.
Enjoy yourself. There is a lot more.
I’ll make myself at home then.
Enjoy yourself. There are plenty here.

ch: B:我吃了好多，不能再吃了。
en: I ate a lot and couldn’t eat any more.
